Acta
v0.2.0 · generated from structured scenario data · regenerate when inputs change
Authority
Working surface only. Canonical state lives in Markdown/JSON and changes solely after explicit acceptance in chat.

Do you understand what the tag-filter change actually did?

The five-slice tag-filter build is merged locally. Before you rely on it, check your model of it against the code that actually landed. This check is diagnostic — it is never a merge gate.

3files changed
+214lines added
−9lines removed

The mental model

Where a catalog render comes from now, versus before the change.

Three behaviors a diff skim won't tell you

Each one is deliberate — the reasoning came out of the build session, not the diff.

01The no-JS page is complete before any script runs

What

index.html ships the full catalog with every tag chip as a plain ?tag= link; app.mjs only re-renders after load.

Why

The spec's no-JS criterion is not a fallback — it is the baseline the script enhances. Chips work as normal navigation without JavaScript.

Where

index.html:41src/app.mjs:12

02Unknown tags render the accepted empty state, not a blank list

What

filterByTag returns [] for unknown tags; the glue renders emptyState(tag) whenever the visible list is empty and a tag is present.

Why

STOP-01 resolved that unknown tags stay empty — no remote suggestions — and interface-directions chose the explain-and-suggest empty state.

Where

src/app.mjs:19src/catalog.mjs:8

03Back and forward re-run the same render path

What

popstate calls the identical renderFromUrl used at load; there is no separate history handler.

Why

One render path means back/forward can never drift from a fresh load of the same URL.

Where

src/app.mjs:27

Prove it to yourself

In progress — answer every question0 answered · score appears as you go

01Where does filterByTag run?

Reveal the answer and why

Answer: In Node tests and the browser — same import

It is a plain export from src/catalog.mjs; node --test imports it directly and app.mjs imports the same file in the browser.

Gap if missed: shared pure-function seam

02With JavaScript disabled, what does ?tag=git show?

Reveal the answer and why

Answer: The full catalog — chips are plain links, filtering needs JS

There is no server; the static page always carries the complete catalog, and filtering is a client-side enhancement.

Gap if missed: no-JS baseline behavior

03What renders for ?tag=deploy (a tag no skill has)?

Reveal the answer and why

Answer: The accepted explain-and-suggest empty state

STOP-01 kept unknown tags empty and interface-directions chose direction B: the empty state names the miss and offers the real tag set.

Gap if missed: unknown-tag fallback

04What state does the page persist between visits?

Reveal the answer and why

Answer: Nothing — the URL is the only state

Persistence was an explicit non-goal at Gate A; the single ?tag= parameter carries all state, which is why back/forward just works.

Gap if missed: URL-as-state non-goal

05Why does popstate reuse renderFromUrl instead of its own handler?

Reveal the answer and why

Answer: So back/forward can never diverge from a fresh load

One render path is the whole invariant: any URL renders identically whether you arrived by load, click, or history traversal.

Gap if missed: single render path invariant

06What was rejected at STOP-01 during the build?

Reveal the answer and why

Answer: Fetching tag suggestions from a remote API

Mid-session scope addition contradicted the approved non-goal (no network calls); it was rejected and the plan continued as specified.

Gap if missed: scope-gate outcome

JavaScript is off, so the answer controls are disabled. Each question still carries its “Reveal” disclosure — answer honestly in your head first, or answer in chat and ask for the key.

Gaps to close

Answer the questions above; any misses collect here as named gaps.
